ground_truth.title: 6 Gardening Shows to Watch for Backyard Inspiration
ground_truth.author: Emily Baron Cadloff
ground_truth.main_content:
As the backyard gardening season starts wrapping up in many areas across the country, now is the perfect time to reflect on how well your garden actually served your needs. Were your tomatoes as fruitful as they could have been or do they need more sunlight? Did you end up with a bumper crop of zucchini and want to cut down for next year? Or maybe you're interested in getting more color into your space and want to figure out which flowers to plant and where. But if you're not sure where to start on your garden redesign, we've got you covered. Here are some of the best shows out there to get you started with a little garden inspiration. Before you get planting, start streaming.

Homegrown on Magnolia Network: Host Jamila Norman is a backyard farming expert, turning rundown patches of grass into urban oases. Norman cultivates crops on her 1.2-acre farm in Atlanta, and with Homegrown, she's spreading the garden love to an even wider audience.

Big Dreams, Small Spaces on Netflix: Host Monty Don helps families across England transform their yards, with each episode spanning a full year to show the realistic work required to create a dream space.

Grow, Cook, Eat on Amazon Prime: Hosts Karen O'Donohoe and Michael Kelly break down how to grow one plant per episode, culminating in the kitchen with cooking tips to bring out natural flavors.

Martha Knows Best on HGTV: Martha Stewart helps people with gardening problems while sharing tips and tricks, working alongside guests outside in a more authentic setting than her typical television appearances.

Backyard Takeover on HGTV: Host Jamie Durie specializes in transforming out-of-control gardens into something manageable and beautiful, tackling big challenges like falling trees and clearing weeds.

Great British Gardens on My5: Host Carol Klein showcases jaw-dropping gardens across Britain, from pastoral villas to castles, providing inspiration for flower gardens with shots of brilliant buds and perfect petals.
with: As the backyard gardening season starts wrapping up in many areas across the country, now is the perfect time to reflect on how well your garden actually served your needs.
If you're looking to take your backyard from frumpy to full-on farming, this is your show.
What makes this show great is the timeline - each episode spans a full year, and viewers get to see the work required to create a dream space.
You don't need a huge plot of land with this approach - with a few planters on a stoop, balcony or windowsill, you can still be a gardener.
Host Carol Klein is an enthusiastic and helpful guide, showcasing jaw-dropping gardens across Britain.

items.caption: Jamila Norman, host of Homegrown, can transform any backyard garden into a fully functioning one. courtesy of Magnolia Network.
